stem: For \( f(x)=x^{2} \) on \( [[[a]],[[b]]] \), find the \( c \) guaranteed by the Mean Value Theorem.
Let \( f(x)=x^{2}-2([[p]])x \). Since \( f([[p]]-[[d]])=f([[p]]+[[d]]) \), Rolle's theorem guarantees some \( c \) in \( ([[p]]-[[d]],\,[[p]]+[[d]]) \) with \( f'(c)=0 \). Find \( c \).
distractor_rationales: This is the right endpoint of the interval, not the critical point.
This is the left endpoint of the interval, not the critical point.
This doubles \( p \) instead of solving \( f'(c)=0 \).
This halves \( p \) instead of solving \( f'(c)=0 \).
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: For \( f(x)=x^{3} \) on \( [0,[[b]]] \), the Mean Value Theorem guarantees some \( c \) in \( (0,[[b]]) \) with \( f'(c)=\dfrac{f([[b]])-f(0)}{[[b]]-0} \). Find \( c \).
solution: \( f'(c)=3c^{2}=\dfrac{[[b]]^{3}}{[[b]]}=[[b]]^{2}\Rightarrow c^{2}=\dfrac{[[b]]^{2}}{3}\Rightarrow c=\sqrt{\dfrac{[[b]]^{2}}{3}}=[[answer]] \) (taking the positive root, since \( c\in(0,[[b]]) \)).
distractor_rationales: This is the midpoint formula for a quadratic MVT, not a cubic.
This drops the factor of 3 inside the root.
This has the 3 in the wrong place (multiplying instead of dividing).
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: For \( f(x)=\dfrac{1}{x} \) on \( [[[a]],[[b]]] \) (with \( [[a]],[[b]]>0 \)), the Mean Value Theorem guarantees some \( c \) in \( ([[a]],[[b]]) \) with \( f'(c)=\dfrac{f([[b]])-f([[a]])}{[[b]]-[[a]]} \). Find \( c \).
solution: \( f'(c)=-\dfrac{1}{c^{2}} \) must equal the secant slope \( -\dfrac{1}{[[a]]\cdot[[b]]} \), so \( c^{2}=[[a]]\cdot[[b]] \) and (taking the positive root in \( ([[a]],[[b]]) \)) \( c=\sqrt{[[a]]\cdot[[b]]}=[[answer]] \), the geometric mean of \( a \) and \( b \).
distractor_rationales: This is the arithmetic mean, the value from the quadratic MVT case, not this rational function.
This is the product \( ab \), not its square root.
This adds \( a \) and \( m \) rather than computing the geometric mean.
This divides \( b \) by \( m \), recovering \( a \), not \( c \).
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
